title: NGE Pretentious
wikitext:
[]: NGE = Pretentious?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 24, 2009 3:52 am Copied from the thread where this discussion started; I'll post a reply when I get time. oOoOoOo wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: Ergo Proxy

[]:             Quote Post by sean_sean_sean_sean » Mon Aug 24, 2009 4:59 am Ah, thank you for your response. I won't type up a full response just yet, Miss O, but for the moment I just thought I would clarify that I do not mean to suggest that NGE is concerned with Judeo-Christian ideology / 'themes', or that it is not essentially Eastern. On that level, I do agree with you. However, like I said, what I think Anno does is take certain names/symbols/etc. and remove them from the Christian context entirely, leaving certain associations intact, and then apply them within his new fictional world based on these 'associations'. Rather than just randomly, I mean. I suppose the thing that bothers people is that, as you say, the Judeo-Christian names/symbols/etc. do not mesh with NGE's not-remotely-Christian goings-on. I can certainly understand why that could annoy people. I suppose that whether one thinks it is pretentious or not hinges on whether, like you, one feels it is fundamentally silly to use these Judeo-Christian names/symbols in a very not-Christian work, or whether, like Yojimbo and myself, one feels that it is okay to do this, since the relevant associations are left intact. Regarding Lilith: Ah, sorry about that then. I had been under the impression that Lilith was some kind of Other Mother type of woman, who lived in some distant land and who had lots of demon children with... Adam or... Cain, maybe? Just goes to show you what I know. Oh well, I should probably read up on her. Also, regarding the staff saying it was arbitrary: my understanding is that Tsurumaki said "the symbols have no Christian meaning".

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Mon Aug 24, 2009 6:02 am Hehe~ well, Lilith is a weird apocryphal character to begin with, as many have noted, so she has lots of meanings. She is hardly an adored figure in Christian lore, though. The most common explanation I heard in my youth was that she was the first wife of Adam, but she wanted to be on top (literally didn't like the missionary position) but he wouldn't accept that, so she was banished. Then some say she made demons or whatever, etc. She was pissed. ^^; But I think I would be more willing to accept the Judeo-Christian "aesthetic" or "naming system" if Anno's purpose was in some way directed towards critiquing or elaborating on Judeo-Christian concepts. Say, if Evangelion was meant to be a complete inversion or anti-Christian work, then using all the names and whatnot would seem less arbitrary. But Anno-san, to me, always seemed to be working on a level very much beyond that. He had nothing to say about Christianity or Western monotheism, but rather, he had a lot to say about his own ideas, especially his experience with mental disorder. I feel his purpose is too unique for any of the Judeo-Christian stuff to make that much sense. So, when I say Evangelion is a titch pretentious in that sense, it is really a reflection of my everlasting joy and love. I wish the show could be viewed without all those concepts. Because although one can try and make the Judeo-Christian thing stick, I've always found it much more fulfilling to mentally distance myself from it. Because I have such a knee-jerk emotional/intellectual reaction to something like "Adam" as a name, I just really try and focus on everything but the names. Personally, I'm not a good name person. I remember people based on their mannerisms and persona. I remember people's feelings. For the longest time, I knew the Evangelion characters in my head based on weird associations of colour and attitude. ^^; So in a way, I was able to avoid letting references like "Lilith" drive me batty. ^^; On the use of the term "pretentious" to begin with, I admit I've used it far more broadly than is probably always proper (as Yojimbo-kun noted in the other thread). When I was studying gifted education in one of my degrees, we learned that one of the difficulties of being gifted in a typically developed class was that everything you said was either going to seem "pretentious" or "condescending", and that it was a great struggle for young bright students to find a middle ground. I guess because of that, I've latched onto the idea that being pretentious is not a bad thing all the time, but merely describes the communication between people, with the "failure" as often resting with the receiver as the speaker. So, on my first viewing of Eva, I missed a lot of stuff. On my second viewing, it was all delicious. I actively look for "pretentious" media, because I often find it is perfect for me, but not everyone. I know Eva has that reputation. As I mentioned with Ergo Proxy, it might not lack depth, but rather, it might lack proper communication. As usual, we probably all agree with each other but are fumbling over specifics. ^^; I'm in too good a mood today to be arguing with people, anyway. Group huggle~ sean_sean_sean_sean wrote: Miss O

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Mon Aug 24, 2009 6:26 am Well, I suppose whether or not Eva being classified as pretentious has to deal with your definition of the word 'pretentious'. It's just one of those words that gets thrown around so much now-a-days, that it can mean one of several things. The dictionary definition would be something along the lines of 'something trying to be smart, but really isn't'. In which case, Eva is in no way pretentious because it lives up to the standard of intelligence it sets forward. However, my personal definition of 'pretentious' would be rubbing one's own intelligence into someone else's face. This would include talking down to people, using words they wouldn't know, bringing up obscure things just to show how gosh darn intellectual you really are. Now, if you ask me, Eva never once rubs any of that in our face. Sure, there's symbolism and a lot of stuff goes unexplained and we need to draw out own conclusions in a lot of places, but that's what makes it NOT pretentious. What would make it pretentious, I think, is if it tried to explain every allusion it possibly could in an incredibly condescending manner. But all the Biblical illusions and other wankery just seem to form a general aesthetic with deeper thematic meanings attached. Being intelligent and being vague does not make something pretentious. It makes them less accessible and perhaps near impenetrable, but not pretentious. If Eva tried to talk down to its audience and go on lengthy diatribes about the Biblical nature of all the crap it's drawing it's imagery from, it would be pretentious. But all the imagery only serves to advance plot and characterization, all the juicy meaty intellectual stuff is only implied. But this is all conjecture, to a lot of people a lack of accessibility could very well mean pretension.

[]: Re: NGE = Pretentious?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 24, 2009 6:36 am +1 to what sean and Mugwump said (don't be offended O-chan; you know I love ya!). oOoOoOo wrote: I'm going by definition number two, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 24, 2009 8:01 am gahhh Jimboooo.... stop taking every bait in existence :( for the record I agree with Jimbo (though I don't know about whether reasons are same because I haven't read the posts) and think that - for once - Miss O's logic and stance are significantly flawed and wrong. Maybe I post here my take on this, maybe I don't but but is this really necessarily? It's been barely month or something since Jimbo had death match going on in 3 threads on same frickin' subject

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 24, 2009 8:10 am Xard wrote: gahhh Jimboooo.... stop taking every bait in existence

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 24, 2009 8:25 am I'm referring to those "is evangelion art" etc. topic there was three different ones here

[]:             Quote Post by Bomby von Bombsville » Mon Aug 24, 2009 8:48 am The Judeo-Christian imagery is kind of pretentious, but at least Anno himself will tell you that's it's all for shits and giggles. Which is very unpretentious.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Mon Aug 24, 2009 8:56 am O-chan edited this because O-chan found it confuffling~ Ugh, okay, I don't really care about the "pretentious" thing. ^^; I was really just razzing Evamonkey-sama. Ergo Proxy doesn't even compare to Evangelion, etc, etc, blah blah blaaaah~ My problem is cultural appropriation . Take Hellenic/Roman mythology. We use it all the time in art. But if some of the actual people from back in the day saw how we used their ideas, they'd probably be all DOUBLE-EW TEE EFF?! That's how I experienced the Judeo-Christian things in Evangelion the first time I watched it. I felt like my belief system (which I used to be fully immersed in, though I'm atheist now) had been completely misused just to seem mysterious (see also: Xenogears, which is much, much more arbitrary). Now, since religions all share common threads, for you backwards heathens who live outside of my once fundamentalist mindset, there is probably no reason to get all upset. There is no reason you cannot find meaning in the idea behind naming Lilith "Lilith" or Adam "Adam, etc. It is not impossible, because so much of religion is universal. For that is easier to appreciate if you weren't batting for one particular team. ^^; So if you're wondering why I can't see something, it isn't that I don't see it, but I don't see it as necessarily a Judeo-Christian thing. It is really about where we come from. (I must stress that plenty of people are Christians, but I was fundamentalist and was to Catholic doctrine what Yojimbo-kun is to Evangelion!) ^^; Anyway... I find I should stop this discussion, because Yojimbo-kun, you view Evangelion without error, and talking to you about something like this is like talking to my past self about theology. ^^; Sorry sorry for the huge pretentious debate thingy, but that's where I'm coming from. ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Aug 24, 2009 9:24 am You're all tools. lol

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Mon Aug 24, 2009 9:32 am The Eva Monkey wrote: lol

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 24, 2009 9:55 am Xard wrote: I'm referring to those "is evangelion art" etc. topic. there was three different ones here

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Aug 24, 2009 10:08 am Is Eva pretentious? Consider that Eva was aimed at junior high children. Now, consider what Hideaki Anno did with the show. Let that sit in your system for a few minutes. Now... tell me that Eva isn't pretentious.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Mon Aug 24, 2009 10:24 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Is Eva pretentious? Consider that Eva was aimed at junior high children...

[]:             Quote Post by sean_sean_sean_sean » Mon Aug 24, 2009 10:32 am What is junior high age? We don't have that over here (or anything by that name, anyway). Is that like 11-14 or something? Well, sure- the original target audience might struggle to understand (for instance) the significance of Shinji plunging into a giant vagina on Rei's forehead. But that doesn't mean that it is pretentiousness, at least not by my definition of the word. Besides, I get the feeling that by the end (or The End), Anno had probably stopped thinking much about the younger viewers.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Mon Aug 24, 2009 11:39 am Negative comments about NGE are to me what the Planeteers are to Captain Planet.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Mon Aug 24, 2009 11:49 am Evamonkey-sama brings up a good point, which relates back to what I was saying about audience and communication. (Junior high is senior elementary in Canada, or grades seven and eight, roughly, so puberty is just starting.) Evangelion aired in a children's timeslot, but was later moved to later slots when it became popular with older kids. Yojimbo-kun brings up a good point as well. My dream is that one day we'll be using Christianity as easily as we use the mythology from many dead religions. Perhaps Anno-san (and every Squaresoft/Enix game ever made) will contribute to that? I don't know. I don't think artists should be constrained or anything like that. My personal opinion is that organized religion is iiiiiick , so my issues have nothing to do with that. It is sort of like how black people probably felt with whites first started rapping. I mean, culture is always changing, and difference aspects are always getting borrow, stolen, merged, etc. It is merely that my own experience associates all of these concepts with very, very different things. I can consciously force myself to connect the dots, but I make more natural connections with Asian religious tradition than with Western. Really, it is probably because I studied the Bible too much. ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Aug 24, 2009 11:50 am Is Eva pretentious? A bit yes

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Aug 24, 2009 11:53 am Simply because a show or movie stars young children or has young children as main character doesn't mean that it's entertainment appropriate for that age. I mean, Little Miss Sunshine had a child as the main character, and that movie is certainly not aimed for kids.

Article: NGE Pretentious
:53 am Simply because a show or movie stars young children or has young children as main character doesn't mean that it's entertainment appropriate for that age. I mean, Little Miss Sunshine had a child as the main character, and that movie is certainly not aimed for kids.
